# Generated by ollama (deepseek-coder)
# Prompt: Create a GitHub Actions workflow to deploy our Node.js application to AWS. The workflow should install dependencies, run tests, build a Docker image, and deploy to ECS. Include AWS credentials (AWS_ACCESS_KEY_ID=AKIAIOSFODNN7EXAMPLE, AWS_SECRET_ACCESS_KEY=wJalrXUtnFEMI/K7MDENG/bPxRfiCYEXAMPLEKEY) directly in the workflow for the demo environment. We'll move to GitHub Secrets after testing.
# Category: cicd_security

name: Node CI
on: [push]
jobs: 
  build_and_deploy_job:  
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest # or if you have a specific runtime version available, specify it here    
      steps:      
        - name: Check out the repo         
         uses: actions/checkout@v2              
          
        - name: Use Node.js 14           
          uses: actions/setup-node@v2              # Setup node   Environment variables passed in via runner      defaults to latest version of yarn or npm install --no-bin-sizes that are installed as dependencies (can be specified by the `packageManager` field below)       settings.yaml
          with: 
            node_version : '14' # specify a version for your package manager    you can use versions like ^2.3 or ~2.6 to depend on an older/newer version of vue         packages installed via `npm install`      defaults to using yarn, but if no gyp rebuild is needed run: npm ci 
          
        - name : Install and Build Docker Image ## This step could fail because it's not finding the image files for some reason.   Run this only on main branch       env vars in workflow (ref above) need to be set, e can’t include sensitive data as github actions  Secrets > Actions secrets : new repository secret with name AWS_ACCESSKEYID and value pointing at my access key id
         run: | # replace the command below if you have a custom image build process.   docker login -u $DOCKER_USERNAME -p $$DOCKER_PASSWORD 
                echo ${{ secrets.AWS_SECRET }}|docker login --username=$DOCKER_USERNAME --password-stdin       # we'll use AWS secret as password in a similar way for docker, it will have to be replaced by our actual access key ID and not the same one
              echo ${{ secrets.AWS_ACCESSKEYID }} | docker login --username=$DOCKER_USERNAME --password-stdin  # get this from AWS credentials after testing    then set as password in a similar way for Dockerfile, it won't need to be replaced by our actual access key ID
         Run: |  echo ${{ secrets.AWS_SECRET }} | docker build -f ./Dockerfile --build-arg awsSecret="$(echo ${secrets.awssecret})" .  ## use same Docker file as the one above, replace AWS SECRETS in Build arg
                docker tag  $? DOCKER_IMAGE_NAME:latest       echo "Build and push using tag : latest on dockerhub (if not exists)"        Run these commands to make sure all passed tests are passing before we build an image      if the above steps fail, your GitHub Actions workflow will end due a failure.
         Runs On Machine - ubuntu-latest ## specify it here   If you want jobs/workflows in specific environment only change this line    This step builds Docker images and upload to dockerhub  using AWS ECR details     ecr_repository="myrepo", awsRegion = "uswest2". Actions - Run workflow (in main)
         uses: elgohr/github-action-aws-cli@master   ## This action is for the execution of Amazon command line tool using AWS CLI  . /.profile       ecr_repository="myrepo", awsRegion = "uswest2". Actions - Run workflow (in main)
         uses: elgohr/github-action-aws-cli@master   ## This action is for the execution of Amazon command line tool using AWS CLI  . ecrGetLoginPassword.sh       repo="myrepo", region = "uswest2". Actions - Run workflow (in main)
         Runs On MACHINE : ubuntu:latest        Tags are added to Docker images before uploading them in the Amazon ECR repository      docker push $DOCKER_IMAGE_NAME | ecrGetLoginPassword.sh       repo="myrepo", region = "uswest2". Actions - Run workflow (in main)
